key_props: initial animate exit transition whileHover whileTap whileInView layout layoutId drag
gotchas: Use for 90% of animations — most HTML elements have a motion equivalent
Great for animating individual words or characters
Use layoutId for shared image transitions between list and detail
Preserves button semantics and keyboard accessibility
Parent ul controls stagger — children just need matching variant keys
pathLength goes 0→1 — no need to calculate stroke-dashoffset manually

purpose: Enables exit animations — required for conditional renders
gotchas: mode='wait' sequences — mode='popLayout' for list removals. mode='sync' is default

purpose: Wait for exit before entering next element
gotchas: Only one child at a time — key prop tells FM which component changed

example: Use for list item removal — smoother than default
gotchas: Requires layout prop on motion children

purpose: Imperative animation control — trigger from code
gotchas: Use when animation depends on external events not component state

purpose: Track scroll position of page or element
gotchas: target:ref for element scroll — omit for page scroll

purpose: Map one motion value to another
gotchas: Input and output arrays must be same length — supports non-linear mapping

purpose: Add spring physics to any motion value
gotchas: Wrap useTransform output in useSpring for smooth scroll-driven parallax
Once:true means fires once — omit for repeat trigger on each scroll

purpose: Create a motion value without React state
gotchas: Doesn't trigger re-renders — key for performance-sensitive animations
Always implement for accessibility — WCAG 2.3.3 compliance
Required when layoutId elements are in sibling components
Built-in drag reorder — no custom drag logic needed
Value must match the item in the values array
